extracted_information: The provided content discusses the challenges of scaling WebSocket servers in real-time applications when handling a growing number of users and thousands of concurrent connections. It highlights the differences between WebSocket and HTTP connections, and the implications for load balancing and horizontal scaling.

features.name: WebSocket Connection Characteristics
features.description: Long-lived and sticky connections, unlike short-lived and stateless HTTP requests. Requires managing thousands of concurrent open sockets per node.

challenges: Clients not receiving messages as usage grows.
Server CPU spikes, leading to crashes.
Load balancers cannot simply discard long-lived connections.
load_balancing_implications: Load balancers cannot simply 'throw away' connections due to their long-lived nature, making traditional load balancing more complex.
clustering_implications: Horizontal scaling requires coordination, and broadcasting across multiple nodes necessitates cross-node communication, implying the need for specific clustering patterns (though none are detailed in this content fragment).
